what_it_does: Consolidates seven parallel explorer waves into a single build-driving research synthesis for the new 'instrument surface' front end, mapping the existing code/data substrate, the bridge API contract, real data inventory, projection geometry, and a proposed fresh design language, then flags corrections to the MANDATE and open decisions for render-based review.
observations: Divided into nine sections (§A–§I) covering addressability, bridge API catalogue, real data inventory, equation/lens catalogue, design tokens, rendering/motion, per-orientation layout, mandate corrections, and open render-decisions.
Cites live code locations (file:line) for inward findings and 2026 design sources for outward proposals.
Catalogues 61+ bridge endpoints across categories including projection, SSE stream, cognition, voice/chat, graph mutation, and operator surface.
Reports real data counts: 6,120 events, 3,038 embedded corpus units, 72 graphs, 18,119 refs, 2,793 vectors, 29 roles, 8 projections, etc.
Defines seed geometry constants: dyadic grid m=2^d, m/2 rings, even angular division x=2π/n, normalized radius band [0.06,1.0], and resonance law.
Lists 12 lens groups (G2–G12) with engine cites, bindings, and operator drives.
Proposes fresh paper-aesthetic tokens: warm neutrals, low-saturation pigments, Fluent-style elevation, modular 1.25 type scale, dyadic spacing.
Recommends SVG+Canvas hybrid rendering with Framer Motion / Motion and a single centralized motion system.
Specifies three authored form-factor layouts (desktop, portrait, landscape) plus a five-state disclosure grammar (peek→hover→open→pin→dismiss).
Includes explicit corrections to the MANDATE/Implementation Guide and a consolidated 'Tim decides by reacting to renders' open list.
standouts: The only permitted UI inheritance is the address-on-UI + context-attach mechanism (L10); all other existing canvas chrome is disposable.
Every interactive element must carry both a canonical `ui://` address AND a Motion `layoutId` to satisfy addressability and no-teleport together.
The bridge exposes a pure-read `/api/projection` as the keystone data contract, with file-discovered bindings making new lenses zero-code-edit.
The real-data inventory is non-toy: thousands of events, corpus units, vectors, refs, and graphs back every visualization.
The seed proportions (dyadic grid, concentric rings, even angular division) are treated as structural law, not tweakable parameters.
conventions: Uses §-prefixed section labels and file:line citations.
Labels recommendations with REUSE / AVOID / CONSTRAINT / OPEN callouts.
Treats design tokens as starting points for render-based reaction, not settled law.
Uses tables for catalogues and inventories.
concerns: Some counts (repo ≈657, history ≈1,892) are inferred from the MANDATE rather than independently re-counted by the data wave.
Two different line ranges are cited for the `/api/stream` SSE handler, requiring reconciliation.
The outward design-language section is explicitly provisional and depends on Tim's reaction to renders.
A large number of 'OPEN' items remain, including exact projection point-field shapes, address_help structure, and aesthetic defaults.
The document is very long and dense, which may make it hard to use as a quick build reference without further extraction.
notes: A dense, cited build-prep map that separates the verified backend substrate from proposed 2026 design language and tells the team exactly what to reuse, avoid, constrain, and decide by showing renders.
questions: What is the exact complete per-point field list returned by `project()` in `runtime/projection.py`?
What is the precise structure of `address_help` returned by the backend?
Which line range is canonical for the `/api/stream` handler in `runtime/bridge.py`?
Were the repo (657) and history (1,892) corpus splits independently verified or only inferred?
How will the Canvas point-cloud layer synchronize animation with the DOM/Motion layer in practice?
Which typeface pairing and motion preset will be selected after render review?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual content: it synthesizes parallel explorer findings into one navigable, build-driving document.
apparent_themes: Addressability as the relational spine of the UI.
Registry-is-truth and fail-loud behavior across the surface.
Dyadic, commensurate geometry as the layout law.
Progressive disclosure and authored form-factor layouts.
Separation of verified substrate from proposed design.
intention_signals: To give the build team a single, constraint-rich blueprint that preserves only the address/context mechanism from the old UI, rebuilds everything else around the seed geometry and real data, and defers all taste/structural calls to Tim's reaction to actual renders.
novelty: Unusually combines a code-grounded inventory of live backend contracts and data counts with research-grounded 2026 design-source proposals and an explicit 'render-react' decision list, all framed as corrections to a governing MANDATE.
contribution: A structured, cited inventory of reusable backend endpoints, data counts, geometric laws, and design-token starting points that a fused build can reference without re-running the exploration waves.
summary_for_embedding: A build-prep research synthesis mapping the existing address/context substrate, bridge API, data inventory, projection geometry, and proposed fresh design tokens for a new instrument surface.
intention_for_embedding: To consolidate parallel explorer findings into a single build-driving reference that separates verified substrate from proposed design and flags decisions for render-based review.
